g.text: rule = exactly one dialect per run
rule = no artifact written until confirmed
rule = confirmed before any purpose-driven default
rule = no silent default (never assume dev- or prod-grade)
rule = read AND write both accounted for before "diagram done"
rule = writer with no admitted path => SURFACE blocking question
scope = every secured store (Key Vault, DB, ACR, remote state)
rule = surface the option (allowlist|in-VNet|tunnel); never bake one in
reaches = vault over a network path the posture admits
identity = operator or CI (whoever runs apply)
role = Key Vault Secrets Officer (data-plane WRITE)
rule = wrong/ambiguous subscription => SURFACE and STOP
managedBy = the skill (runs az login + az account set)
walks = the generated runbook.md, step by step
rule = DRY-RUN must precede any apply
rule = new revision at 0% traffic until health-checked
autonomous = idempotent/reversible steps (RG/ACR create, build, push, plan)
gated = irreversible steps (first apply, secret writes, migration, go-live)
rule = HALT on the first failed step (run no further step)
command = terraform plan / az deployment what-if
rule = produced and shown before any apply
rule = no apply runs without a prior dry-run
scope = production billing multi-factor cutover + traffic routing
PUBLIC_HOST, APPLE_ENVIRONMENT, App Attest prod identity
rule = NEVER auto-confirmed; explicit operator confirmation required
rule = the one stop full-auto never skips
rollout = 0% traffic until health-checked, then go-live cutover
tier = set by DeploymentPurpose (surfaced, not assumed)
holds = every secret (Container Apps secret refs)
order = before any revision traffic routing
The execution engine (CMP-DEPLOY-EXEC). Walks the runbook
AUTONOMOUSLY; irreversible steps and go-live are
confirmation-gated; execution HALTS on the first failed
A plan/what-if must run and be shown before ANY apply.
The one stop full-auto never skips: surfaces the full
production billing multi-factor state, then BLOCKS for
explicit operator confirmation before the live-traffic
Secure-by-default, identical for BOTH dialects: when
Postgres + Azure OpenAI are private, Key Vault is
FULL PARITY: BicepIaC and TerraformIaC compose the same
secured topology and satisfy the same invariant checklist.
